signals.idea: Harden the Solana payment subsystem against the attack vectors found by the adversarial security review. Fix P0: git history secret audit, prompt-injection hardening in admin_chat (remove LLM-sourced wallet addresses from initiate_payment tool; wallets must come from wallet_registry keyed by mentioned Discord user), null recipient_discord_id confirmation bypass (fail-closed). Fix P1: wallet swap window between test and final payment (verify execute_payment uses stored recipient_wallet string; reject or warn on upsert_wallet during active intents), idempotency slot reuse after forced failure (warn/DM on producer_ref collision with prior failed row having different wallet), per-payment and rolling daily USD cap on admin-triggered payouts enforced in PaymentService (default $500/payment, $2000/day/guild, exceeding goes to manual_hold), admin DM on successful admin_chat payouts over threshold (default $100, differentiated from failure DMs). Fix P2: log audit for secret leakage, document RLS posture at top of sql/payments.sql. Already using Helius so RPC attacks out of scope. Two wallets already split into solana_grants and solana_payouts providers. Think carefully about wallet-swap and idempotency — they are subtle and easy to half-fix. Goal is end-to-end hardening.

unresolved_flags.concern: Payments caps: Recurring debt: the revised amount_token fix still keeps derived USD out of the stored payment row, so admin-chat token-denominated payouts do not reliably participate in the rolling daily USD cap or the success-DM threshold.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised Step 8 and Step 9 together against the real admin-chat final payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`. The plan now derives USD for amount_token-only requests during cap checks, but its own assumptions say that derived USD is kept local and not written back to `record['amount_usd']`; because `get_rolling_24h_payout_usd` is defined to sum stored `payment_requests.amount_usd` and the success-DM gate in Step 9 checks `payment.get('amount_usd')`, the actual admin-chat payouts would still miss the rolling daily cap and success-DM threshold in the live flow.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: The amount_token issue is broader than the immediate cap check. In this codebase, admin-chat final payouts are token-denominated at creation time, but later observability and rolling-budget logic operate on the stored `amount_usd` field. Because the revised plan keeps the derived USD local, it fixes the one-request calculation while leaving the broader payment-ledger semantics inconsistent for the same admin payout path.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the real admin payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`; it passes `amount_token` and omits `amount_usd`, so any cap logic written around the grants-style USD caller alone will miss the exact caller the brief cares about most.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: One `must` still overstates what the current plan would prove: 'Cap enforcement covers BOTH amount_usd-supplying callers AND amount_token-only callers' reads as an end-to-end guarantee, but the plan's own assumption that derived USD stays out of the stored row means rolling-daily enforcement and success-DM thresholding are still not fully covered for amount_token-only admin-chat payouts.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: There is still a correctness gap in the amount_token path. The plan derives a transient `cap_usd` for current-request checks but explicitly keeps that value out of `record['amount_usd']`; as a result, later logic that depends on stored USD, namely the rolling-24h aggregation and the confirmed-payment success-DM threshold, would still behave as if admin-chat token-denominated payouts were worth $0.00.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Not all supporting locations are aligned on the amount_token path yet. Step 8 derives USD locally in `PaymentService.request_payment`, Step 7's rolling helper sums stored `payment_requests.amount_usd`, and Step 9's success-DM gate reads `payment.get('amount_usd')`; unless the derived USD is persisted or recomputed in the downstream sites too, the feature is still only partially wired across the repository.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the actual admin payout caller and the downstream consumer. `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365` still creates final payouts with `amount_token` only, while the planned success-DM gate in `PaymentCog` keys off `amount_usd`; with the revised assumption that derived USD stays local instead of being stored, the real caller shape is still not handled end-to-end.

resolved_flags.concern: Wallet registry updates: The proposed `upsert_wallet` blocked-path sentinel is only handled in admin chat, but `grants_cog` is another live caller and would treat a truthy sentinel dict as a successful wallet record.
resolved_flags.resolution: Resolved all 12 open significant flags. (1) Cap enforcement now covers amount_token-only callers (admin_chat final payout): derives USD from provider.get_token_price_usd() and fail-closes to manual_hold if price is unavailable. Added two new tests pinning that path. (2) upsert_wallet blocking is now exception-based (WalletUpdateBlockedError) and both live callers — admin_chat._handle_wallet_received AND grants_cog._start_payment_flow — catch it explicitly, eliminating the truthy-sentinel trap. (3) Collision handling is now index-aware with two explicit branches: Branch A inserts a fresh manual_hold row only when every prior row is failed/cancelled (index permits); Branch B returns None without attempting an insert when a prior row still holds a unique-index slot, and the admin DM fires in both cases. Added a new Branch-B test. (4) Step 5's control flow is fixed: the existing-wallet lookup now runs before the guard, and the sample code matches the real call order. (5) PaymentCog success-DM config is now read inside __init__ from env so both construction sites (main.py:233 and the setup() hook at payment_cog.py:503) stay in sync without touching either caller. (6) Test glue expansion is explicit: inline fakes in test_admin_payments.py and test_social_publish_service.py are named as the sites where has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs must land. (7) Criteria quality: demoted git-history-audit and log-audit from must to should (they are memo tasks, not diff-verifiable); added must criteria for the two new token-path cap tests and the Branch-B collision test. No scope creep; the plan remains aligned with the original P0/P1/P2 brief.

resolved_flags.concern: Payment idempotency: The collision path over-assumes it can insert a new `manual_hold` row for any prior-wallet mismatch, but the active unique index still blocks inserts when the prior row is not `failed` or `cancelled`.

That control flow is not implementable as written and needs to be reordered or rewritten.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The collision path in Step 6 assumes it can create a fresh `manual_hold` row whenever any prior `(producer, producer_ref, is_test)` row has a different wallet, but `sql/payments.sql:126-128` still enforces uniqueness for every status except `failed` and `cancelled`. If the differing prior row is `pending_confirmation`, `queued`, `processing`, `submitted`, `confirmed`, or `manual_hold`, the insert cannot succeed, so the plan needs an explicit non-insert branch instead of assuming a new held row can always be created.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?: The proposed `{'__error__': 'active_payment_or_intent'}` return value does not match the surrounding `DatabaseHandler` convention, which is overwhelmingly `Optional[Dict]` or `bool` with `None`/`False` on failure across `src/common/db_handler.py`. That divergence is risky here because current callers already treat any dict as success.
resolved_flags.resolution: The proposed `{'__error__': 'active_payment_or_intent'}` return value does not match the surrounding `DatabaseHandler` convention, which is overwhelmingly `Optional[Dict]` or `bool` with `None`/`False` on failure across `src/common/db_handler.py`. That divergence is risky here because current callers already treat any dict as success.

resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: The plan adds an admin-chat test for blocked wallet updates, but it does not add any regression coverage for the other live `upsert_wallet` caller in `grants_cog`. That leaves the proposed sentinel-versus-exception choice unverified on a real second caller.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 19.0 -> 13.0. Plan deltas: 61.3%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 9. Open significant flags: 8.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items.criterion: All existing tests in tests/test_admin_payments.py, tests/test_scheduler.py, and tests/test_social_publish_service.py pass unchanged (default None caps preserve existing PaymentService construction).
New test test_confirm_rejects_null_recipient_discord_id passes: confirm_payment with recipient_discord_id=None returns None and logs WARN.
New test verifying privileged_override=True still bypasses the null-recipient check passes.
New test test_confirm_rejects_mismatched_user passes: confirm_payment with a non-matching confirmed_by_user_id returns None.
New test test_request_payment_per_payment_cap_manual_holds passes: amount_usd over per-payment cap lands the row as manual_hold with cap-breach last_error; grants provider is unaffected.

New test test_request_payment_amount_token_path_cap_breach passes: caller supplies amount_token with no amount_usd, derived USD (amount_token * token_price_usd) exceeds the cap → manual_hold. Pins the admin-chat final-payout path.

New test test_request_payment_amount_token_path_missing_price_holds passes: amount_token path with provider returning None/0 for token price → manual_hold with last_error='cap check unavailable'. Fail-closed pin.

New test test_request_payment_rolling_daily_cap_manual_holds passes: rolling 24h aggregation across non-test solana_payouts rows in (pending_confirmation, queued, processing, submitted, confirmed) drives the cap; grants are unaffected.

New test test_slot_reuse_collision_detected_after_failure passes: only prior rows are failed/cancelled with a different wallet → a fresh manual_hold row IS inserted (Branch A) and on_cap_breach fires.

New test test_slot_reuse_collision_blocked_when_prior_active passes: prior row is pending_confirmation/queued/etc with a different wallet → request_payment returns None without attempting an insert (Branch B), the prior row is NOT replaced, and on_cap_breach fires. Pins index-aware branching.
New test test_slot_reuse_same_wallet_creates_fresh_row_after_failure passes: only terminal prior rows with matching wallet → new pending_confirmation row is created (pins reordered early-return).
New test test_idempotent_return_for_nonterminal passes: non-terminal prior row with matching wallet is returned unchanged.

New test test_upsert_wallet_raises_during_active_intent passes: upsert_wallet raises WalletUpdateBlockedError while a non-terminal payment_requests OR admin_payment_intents row exists; admin_chat._handle_wallet_received catches it and surfaces to the admin review channel; grants_cog._start_payment_flow catches it and surfaces to the grant thread.

New test test_upsert_wallet_unblocked_after_terminal passes: upsert_wallet proceeds when only terminal rows exist, and when the incoming address matches the stored address even while an active intent exists.

New test test_admin_success_dm_over_threshold passes: confirmed, non-test, solana_payouts, amount_usd >= threshold triggers _dm_admin_payment_success; under-threshold does not; failure DM path is unchanged.
New test test_execute_payment_uses_stored_wallet passes: execute_payment passes payment['recipient_wallet'] (frozen) to provider.send even when wallet_registry has been updated. Regression pin.
New test test_initiate_payment_tool_rejects_wallet_address_arg passes: wallet_address/recipient_wallet keys in params are popped and ignored; wallet is resolved exclusively via db_handler.get_wallet.
PaymentService constructor accepts per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers, and on_cap_breach kwargs; all default to None so existing construction sites remain compatible.
Cap enforcement in PaymentService runs only in request_payment, never in execute_payment. Scheduler path (claim_due_payment_requests -> execute_payment) is untouched.

Cap enforcement covers BOTH amount_usd-supplying callers AND amount_token-only callers. amount_token-only path derives USD via provider.get_token_price_usd(); if price is unavailable, the row is held as manual_hold (fail-closed).
Cap-breach, collision (Branch A insert and Branch B reject), and null-recipient paths trigger an admin DM via on_cap_breach or equivalent path. No silent rejection.

Collision handling is index-aware: Branch A (prior rows all failed/cancelled) inserts a fresh manual_hold row; Branch B (any prior row in a non-excluded status) returns None without attempting an insert. No code path tries to insert a manual_hold row that would violate uq_payment_requests_active_producer_ref.

upsert_wallet blocked path raises WalletUpdateBlockedError (not a truthy sentinel). Both admin_chat._handle_wallet_received and grants_cog._start_payment_flow catch the exception and surface a user-facing message without continuing to request_payment.

upsert_wallet's existing-wallet lookup runs BEFORE the has_active_payment_or_intent guard so the control flow is implementable. Identical-address upserts and upserts with no prior row are never blocked.
_dm_admin_payment_success is a new sibling method with a distinct prefix ('✅ **Payment Completed**') and no 'requires manual review' line. _dm_admin_payment_failure is unchanged.

PaymentCog reads success-DM threshold and provider set from env inside __init__ with defaults ($100, 'solana_payouts'), so both construction sites (main.py:233 and src/features/payments/payment_cog.py:503 setup hook) stay in sync without modification.

main.py wires per_payment_usd_cap, daily_usd_cap, capped_providers=('solana_payouts',), and on_cap_breach into PaymentService from env vars with defaults $500/$2000 and logs effective caps at startup.
sql/payments.sql has a top-of-file comment block documenting the RLS posture (RLS already enabled, anon/authenticated revoked, service-role bypass). No ALTER TABLE changes.
Admin success DM is provider-scoped and threshold-gated; solana_grants payouts never trigger admin success DMs.

Inline fake-db glue in tests/test_admin_payments.py, tests/test_social_publish_service.py, and tests/conftest.py is extended with has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs additively (no rewrites of existing tests).
pytest tests/ runs green end-to-end.
New cap/collision/DM code paths redact wallets via existing _redact_wallet helpers and never log env values or key material.

has_active_payment_or_intent uses the same non-terminal predicates as existing helpers (('completed','failed','cancelled') for intents; ('confirmed','failed','manual_hold','cancelled') for payments) — no ad-hoc drift.
Inline comment at the collision-detection + idempotent-return reorder explains WHY terminal rows must not short-circuit the early return.
Rolling-24h cap helper includes a comment documenting the residual burst-race window under non-serializable Supabase REST access.
Git history secret audit commands from Step 3 are run and findings recorded in the review memo (pass-through task; not directly diff-verifiable).
Log audit grep from Step 11 is performed and findings recorded. If no real leak path is found, no log_handler.py code change.
Plan output explicitly states that the P0 prompt-injection premise was already substantially satisfied by prior work, so review does not mark prep as incomplete.
No unrelated refactors or test rewrites beyond what the new behavior strictly requires.

In this codebase, admin-chat final payouts are token-denominated at creation time, but later observability and rolling-budget logic operate on the stored `amount_usd` field. Because the revised plan keeps the derived USD local, it fixes the one-request calculation while leaving the broader payment-ledger semantics inconsistent for the same admin payout path.

unresolved_flags.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the real admin payout caller at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:352-365`; it passes `amount_token` and omits `amount_usd`, so any cap logic written around the grants-style USD caller alone will miss the exact caller the brief cares about most.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Resolved all 12 open significant flags. (1) Cap enforcement now covers amount_token-only callers (admin_chat final payout): derives USD from provider.get_token_price_usd() and fail-closes to manual_hold if price is unavailable. Added two new tests pinning that path. (2) upsert_wallet blocking is now exception-based (WalletUpdateBlockedError) and both live callers — admin_chat._handle_wallet_received AND grants_cog._start_payment_flow — catch it explicitly, eliminating the truthy-sentinel trap. (3) Collision handling is now index-aware with two explicit branches: Branch A inserts a fresh manual_hold row only when every prior row is failed/cancelled (index permits); Branch B returns None without attempting an insert when a prior row still holds a unique-index slot, and the admin DM fires in both cases. Added a new Branch-B test. (4) Step 5's control flow is fixed: the existing-wallet lookup now runs before the guard, and the sample code matches the real call order. (5) PaymentCog success-DM config is now read inside __init__ from env so both construction sites (main.py:233 and the setup() hook at payment_cog.py:503) stay in sync without touching either caller. (6) Test glue expansion is explicit: inline fakes in test_admin_payments.py and test_social_publish_service.py are named as the sites where has_active_payment_or_intent, get_rolling_24h_payout_usd, and WalletUpdateBlockedError stubs must land. (7) Criteria quality: demoted git-history-audit and log-audit from must to should (they are memo tasks, not diff-verifiable); added must criteria for the two new token-path cap tests and the Branch-B collision test. No scope creep; the plan remains aligned with the original P0/P1/P2 brief.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: One `must` still overstates what the current plan would prove: 'Cap enforcement covers BOTH amount_usd-supplying callers AND amount_token-only callers' reads as an end-to-end guarantee, but the plan's own assumption that derived USD stays out of the stored row means rolling-daily enforcement and success-DM thresholding are still not fully covered for amount_token-only admin-chat payouts.

unresolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: There is still a correctness gap in the amount_token path. The plan derives a transient `cap_usd` for current-request checks but explicitly keeps that value out of `record['amount_usd']`; as a result, later logic that depends on stored USD, namely the rolling-24h aggregation and the confirmed-payment success-DM threshold, would still behave as if admin-chat token-denominated payouts were worth $0.00.

unresolved_flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Not all supporting locations are aligned on the amount_token path yet. Step 8 derives USD locally in `PaymentService.request_payment`, Step 7's rolling helper sums stored `payment_requests.amount_usd`, and Step 9's success-DM gate reads `payment.get('amount_usd')`; unless the derived USD is persisted or recomputed in the downstream sites too, the feature is still only partially wired across the repository.
